segment-text: EXT. SUBURBAN NEIGHBORHOOD - DAWN

It's dawn somewhere in America. The first hint of sunrise glows on the horizon, barely illuminating the otherwise dark suburban street. Red taillights appear as a garage door opens a few houses down the road. An SUV pulls out onto the street.
EXT. SHOPPING DISTRICT - DAWN
The roads are still empty as the sky turns a lighter shade of violet. The SUV passes local malls and businesses, making its way to the interstate.
EXT. INTERSTATE - DOWNTOWN - SUNRISE
Office lights flicker in the distance as the SUV makes its way out of the city. Sunlight breaks over the horizon, reflecting off buildings' glass facades.
EXT. INTERSTATE - COUNTRY - MORNING

Later, the sun casts an even heat over farmlands. The SUV speeds through the country on a journey that seems to take several hours. Eventually, the flat farmlands give way to foothills. Miles ahead, the hills compound themselves into massive mountains. The SUV disappears into the rising landscape.
EXT. EMMANUEL EPISCOPAL CHURCH - MORNING

The modest red brick Gothic revival church sits on the corner of a small town street. Behind it, built much later, are the church offices and meeting halls. A piano plays inside the church. It's a familiar melody, just unsure of itself. "Hey diddle diddle, the cat and the fiddle, the cow jumped over the moon..." An old beat up Subaru parks out front. JUDY (30's, nervous but well meaning) exits her car, grabs groceries from the back seat and hurries toward the church.
INT. EMMANUEL EPISCOPAL CHURCH - NAVE - MORNING

A YOUNG STUDENT struggles to read sheet music as his PIANO TEACHER guides him through the notes. They play at an upright piano at the foot of the altar. Judy enters at the other end of the nave. She waves to the teacher as she takes a seat in the back pew dropping her groceries down beside her. After praying, she makes a quick sign of the cross. Collecting her things, she waves goodbye to the teacher and makes her way down a set of stairs and into...
INT. EMMANUEL EPISCOPAL CHURCH - BASEMENT - MORNING

The church basement. It's a large but cluttered space. The walls are covered with community flyers and religious plaques. In an open kitchen, ANTHONY (a peculiar young man in his late teens) does dishes.
I'm late. (no response) I know...
Judy drops her things on a table as she rushes across the room and up a small flight of stairs into...
INT. EMMANUEL EPISCOPAL CHURCH - OFFICES - MORNING
The church offices. She checks the phone for messages and turns on a desktop computer.
(calling to Anthony) Has anyone called? No.
(calling back)
No.
No one.
Okay, good.
Judy crosses back to the basement.
INT. EMMANUEL EPISCOPAL CHURCH - BASEMENT - MORNING
Stopping on the stairs, Judy takes a deep breath.
My son had a game this morning but he wanted me to wait till his dad got there, then I got stuck in traffic on the way back... Or some accident...
You got in an accident?
No I got... stuck in the traffic of an accident. (noticing the dishes) What are you doing? Dishes.
Will you help me set up?
What do we need to set up?
The room for the meeting. (then) Why are you doing dishes?
No one cleaned up from this morning.
Oh, I'm sorry...
We got to tell them to clean up after themselves... We did.
Was this AA? No Al-Anon. themselves.
AA clean up after
Just leave it for now, okay? I want it all set up when Kendra gets here.
He stops and dries his hands. He leaves the kitchen and makes his way across the basement, brushing past Judy on his way up the stairs. Thank you...
INT. EMMANUEL EPISCOPAL CHURCH - PARISH HALL - MORNING

It's a light filled room with an assortment of furniture including a wooden table and chairs placed against a wall featuring the artwork of Sunday School students. Judy and Anthony enter, turn on the overhead lights, and survey the room.
Here... let's pull the table out.
They lift the table and waddle it to the center of the room. Anthony waits for Judy. Thinking.
It's so... exposed.
Table cloth?
Definitely not. It shouldn't be so formal.
This looks pretty informal to me.
Let's grab some chairs. I don't want it to look so prepared for them, you know?
She goes to grab two chairs. He follows.
Rev. mm/dd/yy (Blue)
Then why are we preparing it for them?

Because they asked for the space. It's part of our service. (remembering) "Unobserved!" That's what Kendra said. "Unobserved." I think... She had all these requests. (then) I just don't want them to feel like we've thought too much about it.
Little do they know...
Judy looks at the table, suddenly concerned he might be right. In the office, a phone rings. Oh dear...
That must be her.
EXT. EMMANUEL EPISCOPAL CHURCH - STREET - MORNING
KENDRA (early 30's, dressed sharply) closes the door of a rental car. Judy and Anthony walk over from the church to welcome her.
Good morning. Yes!
Are you Judy?
I'm Judy! Are you Kendra?
Kendra Carter, pleasure.
So nice to meet you! I mean, I thought I recognized your voice, but I didn't want to presume... I recognized your voice... That's okay.
Rev. mm/dd/yy (Blue)
And that's okay! Thank you for coming.
Judy goes in for a mildly reciprocated hug.
How was your flight? It was fine. Good.
(beat) Oh, this is Anthony! I'm sorry.
Nice to meet you.
Kendra shakes Anthony's hand.
I think you two have spoken...
Yes. Thank you both for your
(looks around) It's a... very nice church.
Oh, thank you. You want to see inside? (Kendra hesitates) Yeah, sure, we have a minute. Get out of the cold...
Kendra politely follows them inside the church.
INT. EMMANUEL EPISCOPAL CHURCH - NAVE

Now the piano teacher plays. Another familiar tune, but this one without mistakes. The student watches. Judy, Anthony, and Kendra enter from the street. Judy stops to allow Kendra a moment to take in the space.
(obligated) It's nice.
Rev. mm/dd/yy (Blue)
Thank you. We think so. Most people drive north to St. Thomas these days, but... we still find plenty to do.
Where will they be meeting? Oh.
I don't mean to be rude, I just...
No, it's okay. Good question. Downstairs. Just follow me here.
Judy leads them down the stairs.
INT. EMMANUEL EPISCOPAL CHURCH - BASEMENT - MORNING
Judy, Anthony, and Kendra enter the basement from the church stairwell. The piano can be heard from above. Judy leads them through the basement to the offices.
We hold most meetings in here, but Charlotte thought they should have more privacy, so we'll put them in back.
If you think it's best.
Yeah, I think so... (as if it's a deal) I mean, it's much nicer. Lots of light.
And Linda approved?
Oh, well, I haven't actually met Linda before. She's not um... a parishioner.
But she's seen it?
I assume so, she and Charlotte have met here privately a few times, so...
They head up the stairs and into the offices.
INT. EMMANUEL EPISCOPAL CHURCH - OFFICES - MORNING
The three of them turn the corner and head back to the parish hall. Kendra lingers for a moment.
Will we hear the music back here?
Judy and Anthony stop to consider. Oh shoot.
Will that be a problem?
It might be can they stop?
They're having lessons today...
Can't we just close the doors?
I just don't want it to be a distraction.
Of course, we'll talk to them. (back to the room) You want to see back here? Thank you.
They head back to the parish hall.
INT. EMMANUEL EPISCOPAL CHURCH - PARISH HALL
Judy, again, gives Kendra a chance to take in the space.
(re: the room) What do you think?
Rev. mm/dd/yy (Blue)
(still on the music) It's better back here. Oh right...
Kendra moves to the table. She rearranges the chairs so that there are two on either side facing each other.
I was told no one would be in the building is all.
Oh, I'm sorry. Well they normally practice in here but we moved them up to the church...
Okay, will anyone else be here?
The choir's rehearsing for tomorrow.
Oh right... sorry I put the chairs that way...
It's fine. They rehearse up in the church though? (Anthony nods) That should be fine.
(still saddened by the chairs) I'm sorry, we're all a little disorganized with Charlotte leaving on short notice.
Yeah, I'm sorry I won't meet her, she said her mother was sick?
Yes, it's so sad, she has um... early Alzheimers...?
Rev. mm/dd/yy (Blue)
Okay, yeah, and um... it's just awful. She started calling here this week, Charlotte's mom, asking for her husband. He passed over ten years ago.
I know, right? I mean looking for your dead husband? It's scary.
It'd be scary if she found him.
(beat) Anthony, will you go grab some of the snacks I brought? They're downstairs. Maybe lay them out on the far table?
Anthony leaves them alone to get the groceries. Judy mouths "sorry" to Kendra who doesn't seem to mind.
(hopeful) But the room's okay? I think so...
Kendra starts to look around, inspecting the room.
We got some water and bagels. (remembering) Oh coffee! I'll brew some coffee.
That would be nice.
And sorry about the chairs but, how's the table? The table?
The placement? Rev. mm/dd/yy (Blue) CONTINUED: (3)
(confused) It's fine.
(proud of herself) Good. Yeah...
Anthony returns with the groceries.
(to Anthony, proudly) Table's good.
You have some tissue? box...?
Tissue! Yeah, of course. There should be some right here...
Judy crosses to a bookshelf. Anthony lays out food.
Good thinking. Alright, we're getting somewhere...
Judy finds a tissue box. Kendra checks her phone.
How many people are coming again? JUDY Um... why?
There'll be four of them.
You got like lot of food.
Yeah, I don't think they'll be eating... Oh, really?
I just thought...
Anthony raises a large serving container. What is this?
Rev. mm/dd/yy (Pink) CONTINUED: (4)
(maybe a lie) That's my lunch... Charlotte said to have food.
And it's a nice thought, but I don't think we need to have all that out. We shouldn't.
Okay, well, we'll save it.
So how long have you worked with the families? Anthony...
That's okay. And just the one family, but... six years now. Oh wow...
Oh wow...
So... were you... working with them before, or... No.
It's why we met. Oh.
(beat) Have they done this before?
Anthony. That's none of our business. (getting rid of him) You want to start printing out for tomorrow? I did.
Rev. mm/dd/yy (Blue)
Okay, have you folded? Anthony hasn't.
He goes to leave then stops at the door.
Are we going to ring the bell? Judy isn't sure.
She looks at Kendra.
What time do you do that?
We always ring the bell at noon.
(not ideal timing) That's up to you.
Anthony leaves.
Judy crosses back to the table with a
I'm sorry about him. He's fine.
We thought about giving him the day off, but his family... they want him busy.
You don't need to explain.
That's good of you. (then) God knows he's not your typical church employee. Then again we are Episcopalian... (laughs) Sorry. Christian humor.
Judy places the tissues in the center of the table.

Don't put it in the center. (off her look) It's freaky. Put it on the side or maybe... (MORE) (CONTINUED) Rev. mm/dd/yy (Blue) CONTINUED: (6) KENDRA (CONT'D) (re: the counter) Back where it was. So they see it, but not... you know?
Oh, okay... sorry.
As Judy puts the tissues away. Kendra pays closer attention to the artwork in the room - children's art. In particular, FAUX STAINED GLASS TISSUE PAPER HANGINGS by the windows.
So where will you be during all of this? With them? No.
(still looking) They agreed to be alone.
Okay, do you want to wait with us? We got lots of space. Great wifi.
I actually have some calls to make so I'll probably work from my car.
Oh no, that's sad, stay with us. Use Charlotte's office.
Okay, we'll figure it out... (the hangings) Um... what are these?
Oh, aren't those great. That was a school project. They're supposed to be, you know, stained glass... (then, sensing) Is something wrong...
Kendra looks at the rest of the artwork around the room, framed and mounted to the walls. Judy follows her eyes.
(realizing) Rev. mm/dd/yy (Blue)
(beat, then) It's fine. I'm going to step out and make a call, okay?
Okay, sure, yeah... take your time.
Kendra leaves. Judy's left alone, staring sadly at the paper hangings.
EXT. EMMANUEL EPISCOPAL CHURCH - STREET - MORNING
The SUV from earlier comes to a stop at the church intersection.
INT. THE PERRY'S SUV - MORNING
JAY and GAIL PERRY (50's) look nervously at the church.
That must be it...
Gail's eyes are hard, her body unmoving.
Keep driving...
What?
Just... keep driving. Not right now. Not ready yet...
Where should we go? where we are...
I don't know
Anywhere. Just keep driving. (more urgent) Just go that way somewhere and pull over. Just go... Okay.
He drives off, away from the church.
Rev. mm/dd/yy (Blue) EXT. EMMANUEL EPISCOPAL CHURCH - STREET
Kendra exits from the office onto the street. Up the road, the SUV drives away. Kendra makes a phone call as she heads towards her car.

Hi Linda... No problem, I'm here. Take your time.... You spoke with the reverend? Good. I know she's sorry to miss you... Well, I've met the others filling in, yeah. They're fine. You don't have to meet them if you don't want... We'll figure it out.
Kendra opens the car door and grabs her briefcase.

How are you feeling? It's okay to be nervous. You're showing up. How's Richard? Okay. And he's staying with you? I think that's nice, Linda. I do. Alright. And you're walking over now? Okay. Well, it'll be good to see you two... Yeah. See you soon.
She hangs up, takes a deep breath, and looks around at the mountains surrounding her.
INT. THE PERRY'S SUV - MORNING
The SUV is parked at a dirt turn out by a wooden fence that borders a meadow and mountains.
Where the hell are we...?
Across the road, Jay notices a part of the fence is broken. Yellow tape tries to pieces together what's missing, as if some accident happened there. He turns back to Gail.
How you doing?
Rev. mm/dd/yy (Blue)
Gail avoids eye contact.
She doesn't look well.
Gail shakes her head.
I don't know... Okay...
No. I don't think I can... don't think I can do it.
(pause, considering) Well. We don't have to, I guess. We can go. We can leave right now if you want.
No, I mean... I don't think I can say it.
(understanding) Oh, okay... (beat) You don't have to. We can hear what they say, right? But you don't have to do it.
Gail nods weakly, struggling. Jay looks back across the road, something about the landscape haunts him.
If I don't...? (turning to him) Would you?
She looks at him now, expectantly.
Jay is torn.
That's not fair.
It's okay. I don't... No, I wouldn't. (beat, an option) But we can.
He shrugs, almost sweetly, trying to make the best of
(softly) Are you ready to go back? Like it's the hardest question she's ever been asked. Okay...
EXT. SECLUDED STREET - THE PERRY'S SUV - MORNING
The SUV pulls away.
The yellow tape blows in the wind.
EXT. EMMANUEL EPISCOPAL CHURCH - MORNING
The piano music has stopped. Kendra is back inside. The church and street appear to wait in anticipatory silence.
INT. EMMANUEL EPISCOPAL CHURCH - PARISH HALL - MORNING
The room is empty but prepared, water bottles and tissue placed. The coffee finishes brewing with a soft alarm.
INT. EMMANUEL EPISCOPAL CHURCH - OFFICES - MORNING

Kendra works in Charlotte's office on her computer. Anthony folds leaflets for Sunday Mass. Judy enters from the parish hall having tidied up last minute things. As she enters the office she notices the SUV pulling up outside the church. Oh dear...
EXT. EMMANUEL EPISCOPAL CHURCH - FRONT ENTRANCE - MORNING
Jay and Gail exit their car. They wait for one another and then slowly make their way to the office door. Gail stops to take in a little garden beside the entrance.
Mr. and Mrs. Perry?
Rev. mm/dd/yy (Blue)
Hi... yeah, Jay Perry... Gail. Are you Kendra? My wife,
Yes. So nice to meet you in person. Come on in...
Gail...? Gail turns back and heads inside the church.
INT. EMMANUEL EPISCOPAL CHURCH - OFFICES
Gail and Jay have met everyone, but still stand uncomfortably by the door.
Thank you for providing the space. Of course. help...
We're so happy to
We have you all set up back here.
Yeah, there's food and water, coffee. Do you all want something?
(to Gail) I think we're good for now, right? I'm fine.
Okay. Anything you need, you know, you just... let us know. Make yourselves at home. That's right.
Thank you, Judy.
Yeah, thank you.
Sure... (not taking her cue) Would you like me to show you the space?
Jay and Gail look to Kendra for further instructions.
Okay... (taking her cue) Well, it was nice meeting you. We'll be here if you need anything.
Nice meeting you... JUDY
Nice meeting you.
You can follow me.
Kendra leads the Perry's back to the parish hall, leaving Judy and Anthony behind.
INT. EMMANUEL EPISCOPAL CHURCH - PARISH HALL
Kendra walks Jay and Gail into the room. role she let's them take in the space. Now in Judy's
Is the room okay for you?
Yeah, it's fine, thanks.

Good. I'm sorry you'll miss the reverend but we're in good hands here. They've been very nice. (they don't care) Well, I know we've gotten your signatures on everything and you're all squared away as far as our offices are concerned, but while it's just the three of us is there anything you'd like to ask me?
Rev. mm/dd/yy (Blue)
I think we're okay.
Will they be here soon?
I just spoke with them, and they should be here any minute. Oh.
I can check again. Will you be okay if I leave you alone for a minute?
Yeah, I think so, yeah, please, go ahead...
I'll be right back.
She leaves the parish hall. Jay and Gail stay standing, afraid to commit to anything beyond that. Jay watches his wife. She cautiously studies the room.
EXT. EMMANUEL EPISCOPAL CHURCH
Judy and Anthony sit quietly in the pews.
EXT. EMMANUEL EPISCOPAL CHURCH - FRONT ENTRANCE - MORNING
Kendra stands on the sidewalk, waiting. It looks as if she just wanted to be alone, or away from the Perry's. After a moment her phone buzzes and she answers it.
I'm outside. Oh... (turns up the street) I see you...
INT. EMMANUEL EPISCOPAL CHURCH - PARISH HALL - MORNING
Jay and Gail are as we left them. Waiting.
We're right in here...
Kendra enters with RICHARD and LINDA (50's). Linda carries a small flower display. They have a ghostly quality. A kind of expectation to be disliked precedes them. Hello.
Richard, Linda, this is Jay and Gail Perry. Hi...
(to Kendra) We've met before actually.
Of course, I'm sorry.
That's alright. It was... a long time ago.
It's nice to meet you again. How are you?
Fine, thank you.
They greet each other without touching.
I hope this room is comfortable for you all.
Have you been waiting long?
No, we just got here, haven't even sat down... (the room) Is this okay with you guys? It's fine.
Rev. mm/dd/yy (Blue)
He looks at Linda.
Yes, I thought so...
I'm okay. Okay, good.
GAIL
Linda raises the flowers towards Gail.
(unsure of herself) I brought these. For you. Oh... I'm sorry... No.
LINDA
They're...
Small...
(not taking them) No. Thank you. They're... very nice.
Linda cradles the flowers again, embarrassed.
Should we sit?
Yeah, let's have a seat.
The couples take a seat at the table. flowers down in front of her. Linda places the Kendra pulls up a chair to sit with the couples.
Alright... (to the Perry's) We've spoken.].
I think we're set, yeah...

We are, yes. (to the group) Well, I'm really grateful to finally see you all together. May I say that? And I hope, we all feel this was the right thing to do once we've left here today. Is that okay? Yes.
Thank you, Kendra.
Alright. I'll leave you alone then, but if you need anything, let me know. (to Richard and Linda) I'll be staying right in there. (to the Perry's) Nice meeting you both. You too.
She exits to the offices. Her absence, and the silence that comes with it, is devastating. Gail inspects the flowers.
These are nice.
(still embarrassed) Oh, thank you. They... caught my eye I wanted to bring something.
Jay smiles tightly, not sure what to make of them. Gail spins them around and then places them out of her way.
I can take them...
Linda places the flowers out of the way.
(pushing forward) How was your... trip out here...?
Drive? We drove. It was fine. (to Richard) Did you...? Or...?
I flew in last night. with Linda.
Right, okay...
Richard and Linda's separation creates some awkwardness for the four of them. Linda pushes on.
Will you be driving back or...?
We're not sure. We have a room, a hotel arranged, but we may not... Head back?
Want to stay, yeah, right. But... Thank you for agreeing to meet us. And together.
You're welcome. I appreciate you...
She looks to Richard, him having made a trip also.
All of you, making the journey. know I'm not easy to get to...
We're happy to come to you, Linda. Rev. mm/dd/yy (Pink)
Yes. I mean, it wasn't easy... for any of us, I'm sure, to be here. No matter where... Thank you.
Yeah. And I know... we regret some things we've said. Don't be.
Regretful.
We wish we didn't have to rely so much on our attorneys, but...
I understand better now than I did.
We've already given so much, at this point it's really to protect our son. The Perry's shift slightly at the mention of a "son." Richard clarifies.
Yeah.
How is he...?
He's doing well, thank you for asking. They're in Maryland now. Not far from me. They have a boy on the way.
Oh, that's great...
Thank you. (beat) And how is your daughter? Yes.
Rev. mm/dd/yy (Pink)
She's...
She's doing fine.
Yeah... Much better. She's made real progress in the last year. She says she's ready to apply for schools. College.
That's wonderful... For the fall. That's great.
RICHARD
Yeah.
More silence. This is hard. Jay looks at Gail for some support, she's staying silent.
Do we have...? (to Richard and Linda) Did you all bring photos?
Yes...
I know we talked about sharing some... Gail? You want to...? Alright.
Linda, you...?
I brought some other things as well.
Oh yeah, that's right, Kendra told us you might. Okay, good.
(beat) Well, should we...? (off Gail) Or do you want to wait? I'm okay.
We agreed. Here.
Gail searches her purse for photographs.
(offers one photo) Here's Sophie.
Oh.
(taking it) She's beautiful.
(glancing over) She's a young lady now. Yeah.
She's grown up a lot.
(more photos) Here are some of... Gail stops, unable to go through with it.
I'd like to see them. Okay.
Even if they agreed to it, this is hard. This is Evan. three...
When he was
Gail hands over a worn photograph. Linda takes it respectfully. She and Richard look at it somberly. Oh dear...
What's he got there? CONTINUED: (8)
(looking) Oh that was this roller toy, with the balls that bounce... Or pop? They popped, right?
I remember those.
(to Gail) Where's the catcher, the catcher outfit?
(he's asked before) It's here. We brought it... (producing a photo) Here. I think he was twelve. Little league...
Love that photo...
Linda smiles sadly at the boy in the picture.
He was a good athlete, wasn't he?
He was, he was... loved sports. I mean, he... had lots of interests, but, yes he was a very good athlete. (then) He actually didn't want to play catcher, but then loved the gear so...
Jay laughs, then stops, uncomfortable sharing.
(back to Gail) Do we have one of all of us?
(her mood is darkening) Of course. Lots.
She sorts through her collection.
(to the others) We had to print some out. Don't have many real photographs anymore, you know? Yes.
It's nice to hold them, you know, not...?
He lazily motions swiping a phone.
Gail slides a photo across the table.
This was us... Oh that's...
The last Christmas... Yeah... okay.
Richard and Linda look at the photo.
We'd dress up. Cheesy, right? That's what we'd say. Instead of "cheese." "Cheesy..." Kids thought these were, you know... silly.
Linda cries. She isn't consoled.
It's as if they all
RICHARD
(taking the photo back)
Maybe we should...
No, I'm sorry...
It's fine, Linda.
No, I wanted to share too... Okay.
I wanted to share ours, but... It's hard. It is...
LINDA
(beat, aside to Gail) How are you doing? (off her silence) Maybe later?
Gail doesn't look well. She gets up from the table. The others react, but she only grabs the tissue box from the book shelf and gives it to Linda.
Thank you. (wiping, then) If I could just show you...
We don't have to. No...
Maybe it wasn't a good idea.
No, I want to. (to the Perry's) I brought something. It was easier to... Not a photo if... Is that okay?
Yeah, sure, you said.
Right?
(she nods) Okay...
Linda pulls out a mason jar filled with colored paper cut outs, like little origami animals.
JAY
Yes. Something he used to carry around all the time...
Couple of years...
When he was in fourth and fifth grade...?
Younger than that... Okay.
What am I looking at?
Well, he used to love collecting snails... Really...
And he would...
All kinds of things... bugs, spiders, things outside...
Yes, that's true... he loved collecting things outside. (taking over) But snails... Yes.
He was just really fascinated by snails. And he'd collect them in this jar and carry them around wherever he went. To school even, which caused problems...
The teachers didn't want... there was a rule.
You weren't supposed to have animals in class, but they weren't really...
(helping) Sure, yeah...
And kids thought it was weird, but...
It was, a little.
He just loved them. But, yes, kids thought it was... (conceding) And they were messy...
Stephen would, his brother, would... torment them... various ways...
There were problems with keeping them this way, and he would get so upset. When snails... break it's... (brightening) So one day. He showed up with this...
She holds the jar up proudly for the Perry's to see, displaying the objects inside more clearly. Paper snails.

(a rare laugh) Paper snails, yes. And some leaves and a flower. And I think that's a rock. It's a garden. For the snails. And he said, "This way, they won't die." And... I think it's... we thought it was so... clever.
That is... yeah... Or sweet.
(smiles tightly, shaking his head) God...
I thought it was... (then) Well, at the time I had some concerns... You did?
Not like... only about his sensitivity. Really I was just happy for him to....
Find a solution. He was always determined to find a solution.
I was happy that he was.
They fall silent for a beat, staring at the jar. a little ashamed, puts it away.
Well, thank you...
No, thank you for sharing.
I wanted to... show you something.
She looks to Richard for support. He nods.
(changing the subject) So your daughter? She's doing well?
She's doing well. Yes.
Yeah... she's going to be fine. She's so much more social, sociable now, it's... (to Gail, laughs) We miss her now. She's going out too much... That's nice. She's dating.
GAIL
Well, I don't know...
(definitive) She's dating. Okay.
(then) She's really been herself again. Rev. mm/dd/yy (Pink)
She's made a lot of progress.
That's wonderful.
(beat, to Gail) You mentioned, to Linda, you had made some progress yourself? (to Linda) In your letters? A therapist?
Oh, that's right, I'm sorry, I told him...
(reassuring) She just mentioned...
You said you've found someone who's been...
Yes, she's been very helpful. That's right. That's good.
Yes... She was, is, a big reason we're here...
I see her as well...
To meet. Okay.
This way.
Or to talk.
You both see her?
